title: Is the U.S. Losing Its “Backyard” to China?
channel: The Prof G Pod – Scott Galloway

transcript.text: China's tentacles are so deeply embedded
into the economies of Latin America,
South America, and it's just not going
to be that easy for America to take its
hemisphere back. Really, 2026 could not
have got off to a worse start for
Chinese foreign policy. Maduro has been
deposed and the US is saying that it
wants to run not only Venezuela but it
wants to [music] expunge the Western
Hemisphere which basically means uh
Latin America.
>> I think when the dust settles, China is
still going to be involved in Latin
America. It's going to be hard to get it
completely out of uh the US's backyard
as uh Washington would like to see. I've
just been doing a bit of research on how
deep uh Chinese involvement with Latin
America is and I really concur with you
that it's going to be very hard for the
US to counter China in any kind of
comprehensive way. China has already
economically displaced the US in 10 out
of 12 countries in South America,
Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, etc.
China is the primary trading partner of
these countries. It's invested heavily
in infrastructure such as ports, roads,
railways, and power plants. And it's
securing access to crucial resources in
South America as well, such as lithium
and copper.
